text.div: CARDIOLOGY CONSULTATION. REASON: New-onset atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response. HPI: 71yo M with HTN and OSA presenting with palpitations x12h. HR 128 irregular. EKG: AF with RVR, no ischemic changes. Echo: EF 55%, mild LA enlargement, no thrombus. MANAGEMENT: Rate control — metoprolol 25mg TID. Anticoagulation — apixaban 5mg BID (CHA2DS2-VASc = 3). CHADS-VASc: HTN(1), age(1), male(0) — score 2, anticoagulation indicated. PLAN: Repeat TTE in 4 weeks, consider cardioversion if sinus rhythm not restored.
